IMPORTANT DISCLOSURES

This document is for informational purposes only and does not constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy securities. Any such offer can only be made through a qualified offering circular filed with the SEC.

Investing involves risk, including the possible loss of principal. Past performance is not indicative of future results. Returns cited herein are not inclusive of unsold works, which may have lower returns. Art is an illiquid asset class, and investors should be prepared to hold their investment for an extended and indeterminate period.

Masterworks is 'testing the waters' under Regulation A under the Securities Act of 1933. Masterworks will only be able to make sales after an offering statement has been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC") and the SEC has "qualified" the offering statement.

Masterworks Advisers and Masterworks operate as separate legal entities and provide materially different services. Advisory services are provided exclusively to clients who have received the Masterworks Advisers' Form ADV and have executed an advisory agreement with MWA.

MWA AND ITS REPRESENTATIVES ARE SUBJECT TO CONFLICTS OF INTEREST IN CONNECTION WITH THE ADVICE THAT THEY PROVIDE AND ARE EXCLUSIVELY DEDICATED TO PROVIDING ADVISORY SERVICES WITH RESPECT TO MASTERWORKS SECURITIES.

Masterworks art appreciation and correlation data is based on an internal analysis of the repeat-sales index of historical art market prices computed based on a value weighted-basis and focused on the Post-War & Contemporary Art category. All data is calculated from 12/31/1995 to 12/31/2025.

Masterworks often uses art market price data to compare art pricing trends to other asset classes. This data relies on a repeat-sales index of historical art market prices computed based on a value weighted-basis focused on the Post-War and Contemporary Art category.

This communication is sent exclusively from Masterworks and is not endorsed by or affiliated with BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, Amundi, Northern Trust, Apollo, ArtPrice, UBS, or Art Basel.

01 — Executive Summary
THE INVESTMENT
Thesis

Masterworks offers retail, institutional, and accredited investors access to a $58 billion market. The Artprice100© index—tracking the 100 top-selling artists on the art market—grew from a base of 100 in January 2000 to 660 through 2025, outpacing the S&P 500 (402) over the same period, with different risk and return drivers.

The Opportunity

The Artprice100© index simulates a weighted investment in the 100 top-selling artists. From a base of 100 in January 2000, it reached 660 in 2025 versus the S&P 500's 402. In FY2023 the index maintained minimum growth of +1.55%; a slowdown in the high-end segment intensified through 2024, and 2025 saw the first substantial drop in the Artprice100© in eight years. As a distinct asset class with different drivers than public equities, art can provide meaningful portfolio diversification when traditional correlations shift.

The art market's $58 billion annual transaction volume represents a deep, liquid global market, yet institutional participation has historically been limited by high entry costs, asymmetric information, and the absence of a securitization framework.

The Masterworks Solution

Founded in 2017, Masterworks is the first platform to securitize blue-chip artwork under SEC qualification, enabling fractional co-investment in museum-quality pieces by artists like Basquiat, Picasso, Banksy, and Kusama. The platform has facilitated over $1.3 billion in capital raised across 500+ offerings.

To date, Masterworks has realized 28 exits with net annualized returns including 14.6%, 17.6%, and 17.8% on works held over one year, distributing over $68.3M+ back to investors. Past performance is not indicative of future returns.

NOTE: Artprice100© data from artprice.com. Past performance not indicative of future returns.

"The Artprice100© index has outpaced the S&P 500 since 2000, but there has been no way to invest in the products it features. Masterworks is the first company to offer art investment products to the public."
Scott Lynn — Founder & CEO

02 — The Art Market
A $58 BILLION
Global Market

The global art market is composed of auction houses, galleries, advisors, and collectors engaged in the purchase and sale of unique works. Annual sales stood at $57.8 billion by the beginning of 2025, peaking at $68.2 billion in 2022.

Market Momentum

Our analysis shows that the fourth quarter of 2025 marked a signaled return of higher-quality works to auction, improved pricing power, and more competitive bidding depth. Record levels of third-party guarantees provided a safety net for consignors and a floor for prices.

Fall auctions saw record-breaking results, including the most expensive modern art ever sold at auction — $236 million for Klimt's Portrait of Elizabeth Lederer — and the most expensive work by a female artist at $54 million for a Kahlo.

The China Factor

Mainland Chinese HNW collectors spent a median of $650,000 on art in the first half of 2025 — the highest globally. At Christie's through 2024, 44% of buyers in Asia were millennials or younger, shifting demand toward works with long-term cultural value.

The return of Chinese capital is a central pillar of the market's 2026 outlook, moving from a period of pandemic-induced hibernation to one of active, high-value participation.

$236M
Klimt Record Sale
Most expensive modern artwork ever sold at auction, 4Q25
27%
Respondents Planning to Invest
UHNW individuals plan to increase allocations to art and antiques in 2026 vs 65% keeping it the same, per UBS Billionaire Ambitions Survey

03 — Collector Map
GLOBAL DEMAND
Drives Price Discovery

Art is a globally traded asset, priced and transacted across every major financial center. This international demand base reduces exposure to any single currency or economic regime and creates a competitive bidding environment that supports price discovery.

Americas
42%
Market Share
Europe
31%
Market Share
Asia
23%
Market Share
Rest of World
4%
Market Share
"Total billionaire wealth reached $15.8 trillion in 2025, up 13% year-over-year. Only 8% plan to reduce art investments; 27% plan to increase."
UBS Billionaire Ambitions Report, 2025

An estimated $5.9 trillion is expected to transfer to billionaire heirs over the next 15 years, heavily concentrated in the US (~$2.8T), Western Europe (~$1.3T), and India/Asia. This generational wealth transfer represents a structural tailwind for collectible assets with cultural permanence.

10
04 — Art as an Asset Class
PERFORMANCE
Characteristics

The Artprice100© index—designed to simulate the performance of a weighted investment in the 100 top-selling artists—has demonstrated compelling long-term performance versus equities when examined through an institutional lens.

Artprice100© (2026)
734
Index value in 2026; up from 2025 low of 660
S&P 500 (2026)
468
Equity index over the same period (base 100, Jan 2000)
Distinct Risk Drivers

As a distinct asset class with many different drivers than public equities, art has provided meaningful diversification at a time when stock-bond correlations are increasing and seemingly eroding the traditional 60/40 model. The 2025 divergence — Artprice100© down from 720 to 660 while the S&P 500 rose to 402 — highlights the value of uncorrelated return drivers to supplement your portfolio strategy.

Inflation Resilience

As a tangible, globally traded real asset without a currency-specific denomination, art has historically performed well during inflationary and deflationary periods, functioning as a store of wealth across monetary regimes. Fine art serves as a potential inflation hedge: it is priced in a global market across currencies and buyers, so its value is largely protected from dollar devaluation.

* Artprice100© data: artprice.com. Past performance is not indicative of future results.
11
Andy Warhol Unidentified Woman Acrylic on canvas
05 — Structural Drivers
WHY ART
Has These Characteristics
01
Demand Driven by Global Wealth

Art demand coincides with wealth creation among the global ultra-high-net-worth community. As billionaire wealth reached $15.8 trillion in 2025, art market participation expands structurally.

02
Fixed Supply Scarcity

An artist's body of work is finite. As works enter permanent museum collections, available supply contracts further. Less than 3% of artworks pass Masterworks' acquisition diligence.

03
Currency-Agnostic Real Asset

Art is a tangible, mobile store of value without a currency-specific denomination. This global pricing dynamic reduces single-currency exposure and has supported value through periods of dollar weakness.

Historically, periods of Post-War and Contemporary Art market softness are followed by multi-year bull runs with 14% annual median returns.

Masterworks Research
NOTE: The 14% figure reflects the compound annual growth rate of this postwar and contemporary art market following declines in art prices.

"Our mission is to make art investable. So, our operations are built around institutional-grade infrastructure — SEC qualification, robust valuation, custodial insurance, and transparent reporting."
Nigel Glenday — COO, Masterworks

06 — The Masterworks Model
HOW IT
Works
01
Artist Selection & Research

Our research team uses proprietary data to identify artist markets with documented momentum and anticipated long-term demand. Rigorous quantitative frameworks guide selection, blending art-historical analysis with financial modeling.

02
Acquisition & Diligence

The acquisitions team locates works at fair value through a network of galleries, auction houses, private dealers, and direct consignors. Less than 3% of artworks evaluated pass our diligence process. Each acquisition is appraised in-house and with input from independent third-party valuators.

03
SEC Securitization

Each artwork is held by a Delaware LLC. We file and qualify an offering circular with the SEC under Regulation A (Tier 2), allowing both accredited and non-accredited investors to participate. Shares are offered at $20 per share.

04
Professional Storage & Insurance

Artworks are stored in climate-controlled, museum-grade facilities or on loan in museums around the world. The collection is insured under comprehensive fine art policies and terrorism risk coverage, with total insured values exceeding the aggregate offering amounts.

05
Exit & Distribution

Our private sales team interfaces with top collectors globally to sell works at optimal market conditions. Upon sale, net proceeds are distributed pro rata to shareholders. Alternatively, investors may seek liquidity through the unaffiliated secondary trading market.

07 — The Exits
HOW EXITS
Work

Masterworks has completed 28 exits to date, each sold for a profit. Our private sales team actively manages the exit process to optimize timing and pricing.

The Process

When market conditions are favorable, our private sales team engages top collectors, institutions, and auction houses globally. Works may be sold through private treaty, auction consignment, or direct collector placement.

Distribution

Upon sale, net proceeds — after management fees, carry, and transaction costs — are distributed pro rata to all shareholders. Distributions are typically processed within 30–60 days of sale completion.

08 — Portfolio Construction
PORTFOLIO &
Risk Management

Masterworks approaches portfolio construction with the same rigor applied by institutional allocators, combining quantitative analysis with deep art-market expertise.

Artist Diversification

The collection spans 80+ artists across Post-War & Contemporary categories, diversifying idiosyncratic artist risk while concentrating on segments with documented appreciation histories.

Valuation Discipline

Every acquisition is appraised jointly with our in-house team and independent valuators. Our research team maintains proprietary repeat-sales indices and comparable transaction databases covering 3,000,000+ auction transactions.

Insurance & Custody

All works are insured under comprehensive fine art and terrorism risk policies, either stored in climate-controlled, museum-grade facilities or in museums around the globe. Total insured values exceed aggregate offering amounts.

SOURCING
Edge

Masterworks' acquisitions team, led by EVP Masha Golovina (ex-Christie's, ex-Citi), sources works through a proprietary network of galleries, auction houses, private dealers, and direct consignors.

Operating from a dedicated gallery space on Manhattan's Upper East Side, the team maintains relationships with the world's leading art market participants, enabling access to off-market opportunities and preferential pricing.

09 — Research & Analytics
DATA-DRIVEN
Intelligence

Masterworks maintains one of the most comprehensive art market data platforms in the industry, combining proprietary indices with deep analytical frameworks. Major institutions such as Citi and Deloitte leverage our proprietary art market transaction database for their own market research.

Proprietary Indices

Our research team computes repeat-sales and hedonic price indices across artist markets, enabling systematic identification of appreciation trends, market cycles, and relative value opportunities. The Price Database is available to all Masterworks members.

Analytical Framework

Every offering is supported by comprehensive research: artist market analysis, comparable transaction sets, demand/supply dynamics, exhibition and museum activity, gallery representation changes, and macro sensitivity modeling.

"Our research organization blends rigorous quantitative training with an academic foundation in art history — bringing institutional-level analysis to a market that has historically operated on intuition."
Joshua Goldstein — Co-Founder & General Counsel, Masterworks
23
10 — Ways to Invest
INVESTMENT
Pathways
01
Individual Offerings

Invest in individual SEC-qualified offerings representing specific artworks. Each offering is filed under Regulation A (Tier 2) with the SEC. Minimum investment is $15,000 for first-time investors and $500 for returning investors.

02
Diversified Portfolio

The Masterworks Diversified Alternative Strategy, LLC provides exposure to a portfolio of art offerings through a single Reg D investment vehicle. $100K minimum for accredited investors.

03
IRA Investing

Invest in blue-chip art directly through a Traditional or Roth IRA. Masterworks partners with self-directed IRA custodians to enable tax-advantaged art investment.

Secondary Trading Market

Investors may seek liquidity through an unaffiliated secondary trading market for Masterworks shares after the holding period. Trading is available to both US and international investors, subject to a 90-day holding period after initial investment. Liquidity is not guaranteed.

Subject to waiver, a minimum investment is $15,000 for a first time investor and $500 for returning investors. $100K minimum for accredited investors for the Diversified Portfolio.
24
Bridget Riley Blue Quiver Oil on linen
11 — Regulatory Framework
REGULATORY &
Compliance

Masterworks operates within a comprehensive regulatory framework designed to provide investor protections commensurate with institutional standards.

SEC Qualification

Each individual artwork offering is structured as a Delaware LLC and qualified with the SEC under Regulation A (Tier 2). This framework requires audited financials, periodic reporting, and ongoing disclosure obligations.

The Diversified Alternative Strategy operates under Regulation D, available to accredited investors with additional disclosure through the Base PPM.

NOTE: SEC "qualification" only means that the issuer of those shares may make sales of the securities described by the offering statement. It does not mean that the SEC has approved, passed upon the merits of, or passed upon the accuracy or completeness of the information in the offering statement.

Fee Structure

Individual Reg A offerings carry a 1.5% annual management fee, a one-time cash 'expense allocation' payment for each Single-Artwork Issuer, equal to 11% of the purchase price of the artwork, and a 20% profit participation on gains above the offering amount. The Diversified Alternative Strategy carries its own fee schedule as detailed in the applicable Supplement. All fees are fully disclosed in offering materials filed with the SEC.

13 — Competitive Landscape
MARKET
Position

Masterworks is the only platform to have securitized over $1 billion in fine art under SEC qualification, creating a category with no direct institutional competitor.

Structural Advantages

First-mover scale in SEC-qualified art securitization. 500+ offerings create unmatched deal flow, pricing data, and market intelligence. Proprietary acquisition network with access to off-market works. Vertically integrated operations from sourcing to exit.

Competitive Moat

Replicating the Masterworks model requires simultaneously building SEC qualification expertise, art market relationships, research infrastructure, technology platform, and a 1M+ user base. The regulatory and operational complexity creates significant barriers to entry at institutional scale.

Only SEC-Qualified Art Platform
First & Only
First and only firm to securitize $1B+ in fine art under Reg A
User Base
1M+

Helen Frankenthaler Invoke Acrylic on canvas
14 — Macro Tailwinds
THE CASE FOR
Art in 2026

Goldman Sachs coined the defining investment framework of 2026: the HALO effect — Heavy Assets, Low Obsolescence. Their data shows capital-intensive assets whose value derives from physical things that are costly to replicate have outperformed capital-light companies by roughly 35% since the start of 2025. Blue-chip art is the most compelling example of the HALO thesis.

The HALO Effect

Goldman's HALO framework identifies assets whose economic value derives from physical things that are costly to replicate and immune to technological disruption. A Basquiat painting is singular and irreplaceable. While IBM lost 13% on an AI blog post, art continues to recover with Sotheby's posting $7 billion in 2025 sales — up 17%.

Alternatives Stressed

Bitcoin ended 2025 down approximately 6–7% at around $87,000–$88,000. Private equity faced its weakest fundraising year since the early pandemic, with capital raised down approximately 30% versus 2023 highs. Gold and silver have already rallied to historic levels. Art represents a less crowded alternative.

Equity Concentration Risk

40% of the S&P 500 is concentrated in just 5 stocks as of 1Q26. Goldman Sachs projects annualized nominal total returns of approximately 3% through 2034. The CAPE ratio has not been this high since the dot-com boom.

Institutional Consensus

BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, Amundi, Northern Trust, and Apollo all agree: alternatives and real assets are becoming core, not optional. Various industry actors have conviction that the classic 60/40 is evolving toward 50/30/20 models incorporating real assets.

31
15 — Tariff Exemption
ART IN A
Tariff Environment
"Fine art is explicitly exempt from US tariffs — one of the few asset classes with a structural trade advantage written into federal policy."

As the US has expanded tariffs across broad categories of imported goods, fine art has retained a specific exemption under HTS Chapter 97. Paintings, drawings, and original sculptures are not subject to import duties regardless of country of origin — including China, the EU, and other heavily tariffed trading partners.

This matters for investors in two ways. First, it removes a direct cost drag that affects other tangible asset classes. Second, it preserves the global demand base: international collectors can continue moving works freely across borders, maintaining the competitive bidding environment that supports price discovery.

Tariff Rate on Art
0%
HTS Chapter 97 exemption, all countries of origin
Art Market Size
$58B
Annual global transaction volume, 2024
Americas Market Share
42%
Largest single region by transaction volume

While equity markets have repriced to absorb tariff uncertainty, blue-chip art is structurally insulated. Works in the Masterworks collection are held domestically and sold through private treaty and auction — no cross-border import exposure.

32
16 — Common Questions
ADDRESSING
Common Objections
Aren't the fees high?
Masterworks charges a 1.5% annual management fee and 20% carry on gains — similar to a hedge fund structure. All fees are fully disclosed in SEC-filed offering materials. The carry only applies when investors profit, directly aligning Masterworks' incentive with yours. All 28 exits to date have been profitable net of fees.
What if I need my money back early?
Art is a patient capital asset with a 3–10 year target hold. Investors may seek liquidity through an unaffiliated secondary trading market operated by North Capital Private Securities Corporation, available 90 days after initial investment. Liquidity is not guaranteed, but the option exists.
What happens if Masterworks goes bankrupt?
Each artwork is held in a separate Delaware LLC — a bankruptcy-remote SPV. If Masterworks ceased operations, the underlying assets would remain the property of shareholders in each offering. The platform's financial health does not affect ownership of the art itself.
What if the art market drops?
Post-War and Contemporary Art downturns have historically been followed by multi-year recoveries with median annual returns of 14%. Supply is also structurally thinning — as works enter permanent museum collections, available inventory contracts, which supports long-term price floors.
How is this different from buying art directly?
Direct collectors need $500K+ to access museum-quality works, bear full carrying costs, and face illiquid, opaque markets. Masterworks offers fractional ownership starting at $15K, SEC-qualified structures, professional management, and a secondary market — with the same underlying asset appreciation potential.

Experience

All members receive access to the Masterworks Price Database — a comprehensive time-series tool showing historical auction data, repeat-sale pairs, artist metrics, and market analytics across the contemporary art market.

18 — Leadership
EXECUTIVE
Team
Scott Lynn
Founder & Chief Executive Officer

Serial entrepreneur who previously founded Treeloot (mobile gaming, backed by Intel Capital), Adknowledge (grown to $300M+ in revenue), and Payability (processed $6B+ in financing). Personal art collection ranked among the Top 100 in the US, with works lent to the Museum of Modern Art and the Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum.

Nigel Glenday
Chief Operating Officer

Scaled operations for the $1.2B publicly securitized art collection. Career spanning specialty finance, corporate strategy, and investment banking. Previously Managing Director for Strategy at Athena Art Finance (Carlyle Group) and financial institutions banker at UBS and Morgan Stanley.

Masha Golovina
EVP & Head of Acquisitions

Built Masterworks' acquisitions function from the ground up as the first hire for market research and acquisitions. Oversees sourcing strategy, valuation discipline, process development, and execution. Previously Associate Vice President at Christie's and in portfolio strategy at Citi.

Joshua Goldstein
Co-Founder & General Counsel

Co-Founder of Masterworks. Developed and oversees the legal framework for 500+ Regulation A offerings, pioneering a new category of securitized alternative assets. Previously EVP & Chief General Counsel at Intrawest, where he led the NYSE IPO. Earlier career at Skadden Arps.
